params.name: The Selfish Gene
params.description: The Selfish Gene is a popular science book by British evolutionary biologist Richard Dawkins, which first proposed that the selfish nature of genes is the core driving force of evolution. The book points out that organisms are merely survival machines for genes, which achieve their replication and dissemination by controlling biological behavior. It also explores how the selfishness of genes influences biological behavior, competition, and reproductive strategies, extending even to explanations  …
